title: FDA Expands Warning Letters Over Compounded GLP-1 Marketing Claims
excerpt: Federal regulators said several telehealth and compounding operators made unsupported claims around semaglutide and tirzepatide access, adding fresh pressure to peptide-adjacent clinics.

title: Biotech Analysts Lift Peptide Therapeutics Outlook After Strong Obesity Drug Demand
excerpt: Market watchers pointed to GLP-1 demand, manufacturing expansion, and investor interest in next-generation peptide therapeutics as signs of a broader category repricing.

title: Early Tendon Repair Study Revives Discussion Around BPC-157 Evidence Gaps
excerpt: Researchers cautioned that promising preclinical findings around BPC-157 still need larger human trials before clinicians can draw firm conclusions for sports medicine.

title: Compounders Report Raw Material Delays for Popular Research Peptides
excerpt: Sourcing teams described longer lead times for lyophilized peptide inputs, including GHK-Cu and CJC-1295, as quality testing requirements tighten.

title: Patent Dispute Puts Next-Generation Amylin Peptide Program Under Review
excerpt: A new filing claims overlapping intellectual property around a metabolic peptide candidate, raising questions for licensing talks and future trial timelines.

title: Wellness Clinics Add More Screening Before Peptide Therapy Consults
excerpt: Several hormone optimization clinics said new intake protocols are designed to flag medication interactions and steer consumers away from unverified research peptide use.

title: Recall Notice Cites Contamination Risk in Unapproved Peptide Vials
excerpt: A distributor pulled several lots after testing found sterility concerns in injectable products advertised for laboratory research and bodybuilding communities.

title: Clinical Trial Explores Tesamorelin for Inflammatory Liver Biomarkers
excerpt: Investigators are tracking whether tesamorelin can improve selected metabolic and inflammatory markers in a small randomized clinical study.

title: Semaglutide Supply Improves as Fill-Finish Capacity Comes Online
excerpt: Manufacturers said new capacity should help stabilize supply for several GLP-1 products, though pharmacies still expect localized shortages through the quarter.

title: Longevity Conference Highlights GHK-Cu, Epitalon, and Evidence Standards
excerpt: Panelists urged clinicians and consumers to separate mechanistic curiosity from clinical proof as biohacking interest in peptide protocols continues to rise.

title: Telehealth Peptide Prescriber Faces Federal Charges Over Import Scheme
excerpt: Prosecutors allege the prescriber helped route unapproved research chemical shipments to patients without required safety documentation.

title: New Funding Round Targets Oral Peptide Delivery Platform
excerpt: The startup says its capsule technology could improve adherence for peptide therapeutics that are traditionally delivered by subcutaneous injection.

title: Researchers Compare Selank and Semax Mechanisms in Stress Models
excerpt: A review article summarizes how nootropic peptide candidates may influence signaling pathways, while noting limited human evidence and uneven study quality.

title: State Board Opens Review of Compounding Pharmacy Peptide Ads
excerpt: Regulators are reviewing whether promotional language around peptide therapy crossed state advertising rules for pharmacies and supervising clinicians.

title: Manufacturers Expand Peptide API Testing After Import Scrutiny
excerpt: Quality teams are increasing identity and purity testing for peptide active pharmaceutical ingredients after customs officials flagged documentation gaps.

title: Bremelanotide Label Discussion Draws Renewed Attention From Clinicians
excerpt: Clinicians say PT-141 education still needs clearer patient screening guidance, especially for cardiovascular risk factors and off-label wellness messaging.
